zotero.abstractNote: Planning in complex environments with sparse rewards is a major challenge in reinforcement learning. Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) is a powerful tool for planning, but its performance degrades in long-horizon tasks. We propose Divide-and-Conquer MCTS (DC-MCTS), a hierarchical planning algorithm that decomposes a long-horizon goal-directed task into a sequence of subgoals. DC-MCTS learns a goal-conditioned policy and a value function to guide the search, and uses a divide-and-conquer strategy to recursively break down the planning problem. We evaluate DC-MCTS on a set of challenging navigation and manipulation tasks, and show that it significantly outperforms standard MCTS and other baselines.
